q: Only those who dare to fail greatly can ever achieve greatly.
h:
“Only those who dare to fail greatly can ever achieve greatly.” —
Robert F. Kennedy

q: The dreamers are the saviors of the worlds.
h:
“The dreamers are the saviors of the worlds.” —
James Allen

q: It's your outlook on life that counts.
h:
“It's your outlook on life that counts.” —
Betty White

q: Life isn't about finding yourself. Life is about creating yourself.
h:
“Life isn't about finding yourself. Life is about creating yourself.” —
George Bernard Shaw

q: All has not been said and never will be.
h:
“All has not been said and never will be.” —
Samuel Beckett

q: Confidence is contagious. So is lack of confidence.
h:
“Confidence is contagious. So is lack of confidence.” —
Vince Lombardi

q: You are free, and that is why you are lost.
h:
“You are free, and that is why you are lost. ” —
Franz Kafka

q: As you think, you travel, and as you love, you attract.
h:
“As you think, you travel, and as you love, you attract.” —
James Allen

q: The person who says something is impossible should not interrupt the person who is doing it.
h:
“The person who says something is impossible should not interrupt the person who is doing it.” —
Chinese Proverb

q: Success is an iceberg.
h:
“Success is an iceberg.” —
Unknown

q: Don't let anyone define you. You define yourself.
h:
“Don't let anyone define you. You define yourself.” —
Billie Jean King

q: If you want to turn a vision into reality, you have to give 100% and never stop believing in your dream.
h:
“If you want to turn a vision into reality, you have to give 100% and never stop believing in your dream.” —
Arnold Schwarzenegger

q: There are no second chances in life, except to feel remorse.
h:
“There are no second chances in life, except to feel remorse.” —
Carlos Ruiz Zafon

q: Sorrow is how we learn to love.
h:
“Sorrow is how we learn to love.” —
Rita Mae Brown

q: If you tell the truth, you don't have to remember anything.
h:
“If you tell the truth, you don't have to remember anything.” —
Mark Twain

q: A woman unsatisfied must have luxuries. But a woman who loves a man would sleep on a board.
h:
“A woman unsatisfied must have luxuries. But a woman who loves a man would sleep on a board.” —
D. H. Lawrence

q: Feelings come and go like clouds in a windy sky. Conscious breathing is my anchor.
h:
“Feelings come and go like clouds in a windy sky. Conscious breathing is my anchor.” —
Thich Nhat Hanh

q: You must conceive it in your heart and mind before you can receive it. If you believe then all things are possible.
h:
“You must conceive it in your heart and mind before you can receive it. If you believe then all things are possible.” —
Norman Vincent Peale

q: Success is the sum of small efforts, repeated day in and day out.
h:
“Success is the sum of small efforts, repeated day in and day out.” —
Robert Collier

q: To be calm is the highest achievement of the self.
h:
“To be calm is the highest achievement of the self.” —
Zen Proverb

q: Of all men's miseries the bitterest is this: to know so much and to have control over nothing.
h:
“Of all men's miseries the bitterest is this: to know so much and to have control over nothing. ” —
Herodotus

q: If you put water into a cup, it becomes the cup.
h:
“If you put water into a cup, it becomes the cup.” —
Bruce Lee

q: Go and do the things you can't. That is how you get to do them.
h:
“Go and do the things you can't. That is how you get to do them.” —
Pablo Picasso

q: To be happy we need something to solve. Happiness is therefore a form of action.
h:
“To be happy we need something to solve. Happiness is therefore a form of action.” —
Mark Manson

q: Rest when you're weary. Refresh and renew yourself, your body, your mind, your spirit. Then get back to work.
h:
“Rest when you're weary. Refresh and renew yourself, your body, your mind, your spirit. Then get back to work.” —
Ralph Marston

q: You must find the place inside yourself where nothing is impossible.
h:
“You must find the place inside yourself where nothing is impossible.” —
Deepak Chopra

q: The best way to predict the future is to create it.
h:
“The best way to predict the future is to create it.” —
Abraham Lincoln

q: Create with the heart; build with the mind.
h:
“Create with the heart; build with the mind.” —
Criss Jami

q: It is indeed a radical act of love just to sit down and be quiet for a time by yourself.
h:
“It is indeed a radical act of love just to sit down and be quiet for a time by yourself.” —
Jon Kabat-Zinn

q: Muddy water is best cleared by leaving it alone.
h:
“Muddy water is best cleared by leaving it alone.” —
Alan Watts

q: You're braver than you believe, and stronger than you seem, and smarter than you think.
h:
“You're braver than you believe, and stronger than you seem, and smarter than you think.” —
A.A. Milne

q: To be fully alive, fully human, and completely awake is to be continually thrown out of the nest.
h:
“To be fully alive, fully human, and completely awake is to be continually thrown out of the nest.” —
Pema Chodron

q: What have you done today to make someone else happy?
h:
“What have you done today to make someone else happy?” —
Deepam Chaterjee

q: Those who dream by day are cognizant of many things which escape those who dream only by night.
h:
“Those who dream by day are cognizant of many things which escape those who dream only by night.” —
Vincent van Gogh

q: If we will be quiet and ready enough, we shall find compensation in every disappointment.
h:
“If we will be quiet and ready enough, we shall find compensation in every disappointment.” —
Henry David Thoreau

q: If fear is too strong, the genius is suppressed.
h:
“If fear is too strong, the genius is suppressed.” —
Robert Kiyosaki

q: In the End, we will remember not the words of our enemies, but the silence of our friends.
h:
“In the End, we will remember not the words of our enemies, but the silence of our friends.” —
Martin Luther King, Jr.

q: Instead of worrying about what you cannot control, shift your energy to what you can create.
h:
“Instead of worrying about what you cannot control, shift your energy to what you can create.” —
Roy T. Bennett

q: The truth is simple. If it was complicated, everyone would understand it.
h:
“The truth is simple. If it was complicated, everyone would understand it.” —
Walt Whitman

q: Be confident, not certain.
h:
“Be confident, not certain.” —
Eleanor Roosevelt

q: There are more people who wish to be loved than there are who are willing to love.
h:
“There are more people who wish to be loved than there are who are willing to love. ” —
Nicolas Chamfort

q: No valid plans for the future can be made by those who have no capacity for living now.
h:
“No valid plans for the future can be made by those who have no capacity for living now.” —
Alan Watts

q: Realize deeply that the present moment is all you have. Make the NOW the primary focus of your life.
h:
“Realize deeply that the present moment is all you have. Make the NOW the primary focus of your life. ” —
Eckhart Tolle

q: Poetry is written with tears, fiction with blood, and history with invisible ink.
h:
“Poetry is written with tears, fiction with blood, and history with invisible ink.” —
Carlos Ruiz Zafon

q: A fear-oriented man is always calculating, planning, arranging, safeguarding. His whole life is lost in this way.
h:
“A fear-oriented man is always calculating, planning, arranging, safeguarding. His whole life is lost in this way.” —
Osho

q: Show kindness but never expect it. Show authenticity but never expect it. Only the wise are indifferent to things they can't control.
h:
“Show kindness but never expect it. Show authenticity but never expect it. Only the wise are indifferent to things they can't control.” —
Maxime Lagace

q: Everything eaten is killed. Every meal is a sacrifice.
h:
“Everything eaten is killed. Every meal is a sacrifice.” —
Adi Da Samraj

q: Fear can hold you prisoner. Hope can set you free.
h:
“Fear can hold you prisoner. Hope can set you free.” —
Stephen King

q: What loneliness is more lonely than distrust?
h:
“What loneliness is more lonely than distrust?” —
George Eliot

q: The best revenge is to be unlike him who performed the injury.
h:
“The best revenge is to be unlike him who performed the injury.” —
Marcus Aurelius
